zh.lifeSummary: 當Marie de BRABANT於大約1278年出生在比利時福拉阿姆-布拉班特洛文時，她的父親Jan I hertog van Brabant27歲，母親Marguerite de Dampierre30歲。她於1297年10月23日與Amédée de SAVOIE IV結婚。他們至少是5個女兒的父母。她去世於1338年11月2日在法國奧維-o-阿爾培斯薩伏伊沙吉讓安-德-莫瑞安享年61歲，埋葬在比利時布拉班特布波瑟爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Marie de BRABANT于大约1278年出生在比利时福拉阿姆-布拉班特洛文时，她的父亲Jan I hertog van Brabant27岁，母亲Marguerite de Dampierre30岁。她于1297年10月23日与Amédée de SAVOIE IV结婚。他们至少是5个女儿的父母。她去世于1338年11月2日在法国奧維-o-阿爾培斯薩伏伊沙吉讓安-德-莫瑞安享年61岁，埋葬在比利时布拉班特布波瑟爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: BRABANT
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aime, Aristide, Marcel, Normand, Ovila, Yvan.
French, Walloon, Flemish, and German: habitational name from Brabant, a province in the Low Countries.

English: cognate of 1 above, from Old French Brabant, Middle English Braban, Brabayn, used to denote both the region and an inhabitant of it. See also the synonymous Brabazon . In medieval England, Brabans were typically linen weavers and cloth merchants. The final syllable of Braban, when weakly stressed, was often altered to -ben, -bin, or -bon, and also to -bam. The additional -t or -d in post-medieval Brabant, Braband , etc. is probably excrescent, rather than a relic of the Old French form.

variants.name: Brabender
meanings: German (mainly Westphalia): habitational name for someone from Brabant (see   Brabant  ), perhaps applied also as a nickname for a merchant of Brabantine cloth.

variants.name: Brabazon
meanings: English: from Old French Brabançon, Anglo-Norman French Brabanzon, Middle English Brab(a)son ‘Brabanter’. This was originally an ethnic name for a native of the duchy of Brabant (now in north Belgium). Medieval Brabant was widely known for two exports, its brutal mercenary soldiers and its linen cloth. In the 13th century parts of France were devastated by marauding gangs of armed Brabanters, and brabançon came to be used generically to denote a mercenary, hired assassin, or robber. These originated in Brabant and Flanders, but in the course of time accepted recruits from anywhere. The true source of the surname is the presence in many English (and Irish) towns of Brabant cloth merchants.

variants.name: Bremner
meanings: Scottish: habitational name for someone from Brabant in the Low Countries, from Older Scots Brebner, Brabanare ‘native or inhabitant of Brabant’ (see Brabant and compare with the synonymous Brabazon ). Weavers from Brabant settled early at Aberdeen and on the east coast of Scotland.
German: habitational name for someone from the city of Bremen. This surname is very rare in Germany.

variants.name: Brabham
meanings: English: variant of Brabant , altered by association with English habitational names ending in -ham.

variants.name: Braband
meanings: German and English: variant of   Brabant  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Marie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of Maria . When first introduced to England in the Middle Ages, it was Anglicized in pronunciation and respelled Mary . This French form was reintroduced into the English-speaking world as a separate name in the 19th century, and is still pronounced more or less in the French manner, although sometimes with the stress on the first syllable. It is now often used in combination with other names such as Ellie, Chloe, and Lisa.

en.lifeSummary: When Marie de BRABANT was born about 1278, in Leuven, Flemish Brabant, Belgium, her father, Jan I hertog van Brabant, was 27 and her mother, Marguerite de Dampierre, was 30. She married Amédée de SAVOIE IV on 23 October 1297. They were the parents of at least 5 daughters. She died on 2 November 1338, in Saint-Jean-de-Maurienne, Savoie, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France, at the age of 61, and was buried in Brussels, Brabant, Belgium.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Marie de BRABANT est née environ 1278, à Leuven, Brabant Flamand, Belgique, son père, Jan I hertog van Brabant, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Marguerite de Dampierre, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Amédée de SAVOIE IV le 23 octobre 1297. Ils ont eu au moins 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 2 novembre 1338, à Saint-Jean-de-Maurienne, Savoie, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterrée à Bruxelles, Brabant, Belgique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Marie de BRABANT um 1278 in Leuven, Flämisch Brabant, Belgien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Jan I hertog van Brabant 27 und ihre Mutter Marguerite de Dampierre 30. Sie hat am 23. Oktober 1297 Amédée de SAVOIE IV geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Töchter. Sie starb am 2. November 1338 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Saint-Jean-de-Maurienne, Savoie, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich und wurde in Brüssel, Brabant, Belgien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Marie de BRABANT è nata il circa 1278 nel Leuven, Vlaams-Brabant, Belgio, sua padre, Jan I hertog van Brabant, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Marguerite de Dampierre, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato Amédée de SAVOIE IV il 23 ottobre 1297. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figlie. Morì il 2 novembre 1338, nel Saint-Jean-de-Maurienne, Savoie, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia, all'età di 61 anni, e fu sepolta a Brussel, Brabante, Belgio.

ja.lifeSummary: Marie de BRABANTが約1278年にベルギーvラアムス-ブラバントレウベンで生まれた時，父Jan I hertog van Brabantは27歳，母Marguerite de Dampierreは30歳であった。1297年10月23日にAmédée de SAVOIE IVと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1338年11月2日にフランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスサボイサイント-ジェアン-デ-マアウリーンで61歳で死去し，ベルギーブラバントブルセルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Marie de BRABANT(이)가 대략 1278년 벨기에 블라아스-브라반트 레우븐에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Jan I hertog van Brabant(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Marguerite de Dampierre(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1297년 10월 23일 Amédée de SAVOIE IV(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1338년 11월 2일 프랑스 오버그니-ho니-알프스 서아보이 세인트-j이-데-모리니에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 벨기에 브라반트 브루셀에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Marie de BRABANT nasceu aproximadamente 1278, em Leuven, Vlaams-Brabant, Bélgica, seu pai, Jan I hertog van Brabant, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Marguerite de Dampierre, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Amédée de SAVOIE IV em 23 de outubro de 1297. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 2 de novembro de 1338, em Saint-Jean-de-Maurienne, Savoie, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, França, com 61 anos, e foi sepultada em Bruxelas, Brabante, Bélgica.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Marie de BRABANT родилась около 1278 г. в местности леувен, влаамс-брабант, Бельгия, её отцу по имени Jan I hertog van Brabant было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Marguerite de Dampierre, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Amédée de SAVOIE IV 23 октября 1297 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 дочерей. Она умерла 2 ноября 1338 г. в местности сеинт-джин-де-мауриенн, савои, аувиргне-рoне-алпесь, Франция в возрасте 61 год и была похоронена в местности брассел, брабант, Бельгия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Marie de BRABANT nació aproximadamente 1278, en Leuven, Vlaams-Brabant, Bélgica, su papá, Jan I hertog van Brabant, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Marguerite de Dampierre, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Amédée de SAVOIE IV el 23 de octubre de 1297. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijas. Murió el 2 de noviembre de 1338, en Saint-Jean-de-Maurienne, Savoie, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultada en Brussel, Brabante, Bélgica.
